Cenaculum
Family of Saint Jerome Familia Sancti Hieronymi ENG LAT
About the community

Family of Saint Jerome

The community that supports Cenaculum and related apostolates of prayer, Latin liturgy, hospitality, and friendship.

Back to Cenaculum
De communitate

Familia Sancti Hieronymi

Communitas quae Cenaculum atque alia apostolata precationis, liturgiae Latinae, hospitalitatis, et amicitiae sustinet.

Ad Cenaculum redi
What it is

What the Family is for

Prayer and formation

The Family of Saint Jerome exists to help Catholics live the faith more deeply, with a particular love for Latin prayer, the liturgy, and faithful formation.

Hospitality

Cenaculum is one of the chief ways this life is made visible: a place where prayer, study, meals, and conversation are shared patiently and concretely.

Friendship

The community is not only about events. It is also about friendship that continues before and after Cenaculum, across years and places.

Quid sit

Cui rei Familia sit

Precatio et formatio

Familia Sancti Hieronymi instituta est ut fideles ad vitam christianam altius ducendam adiuvet, cum peculiari amore erga precationem Latinam, liturgiam, atque formationem fidelem.

Hospitalitas

Cenaculum una ex praecipuis viis est quibus haec vita conspicua fit: locus ubi precatio, studium, cibus, atque sermo patienter et concrete communicantur.

Amicitia

Communitas non solum de eventibus agit. De amicitia etiam agit, quae ante Cenaculum et post Cenaculum, per annos et per loca, perseverat.

History

A brief history

The Family of Saint Jerome grew out of a desire to keep Latin prayer, Latin liturgy, and Catholic learning close together. Cenaculum developed as a natural expression of that vision, and the community continues to gather around it each year.

Historia

Brevis historia

Familia Sancti Hieronymi ex desiderio orta est precationem Latinam, liturgiam Latinam, atque studia catholica inter se coniungere. Cenaculum tamquam expressio naturalis huius visionis paulatim evolutum est, et communitas circa illud quotannis convenire pergit.
